content:
{
  "title": "Language-Agnostic Prompt Interpolation: Enabling Zero-shot Transfer to Low-Resource Languages",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) often underperform on low-resource languages due to limited training exposure and prompting misalignment. Existing prompting strategies fail to bridge the gap between high-resource and truly low-resource languages in zero-shot settings.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are deployed globally, equitable performance across languages is essential but remains elusive, especially for communities speaking underrepresented languages. Prompting presents a lightweight, scalable solution, yet current methods lack effective mechanisms for cross-lingual transfer. Unlocking universal prompt templates could democratize LLM capabilities for all languages, not just the well-resourced.",
  "method": "We propose Language-Agnostic Prompt Interpolation (LAPI): prompts for a target low-resource language are generated by interpolating between (i) their machine-translation-based counterparts and (ii) language-agnostic meta-prompts derived from high-resource languages using a learned embedding alignment. During inference, LAPI dynamically blends both prompt types using a similarity-based weighting informed by language typology and available unlabeled data. This approach exploits shared semantics while adapting to language-specific features, requiring no labeled examples in the target language.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate LAPI on cross-lingual QA (TyDi QA, XQuAD) and NLU tasks (XTREME), targeting languages with minimal training data. Baselines include vanilla translation prompts, retrieval-augmented prompting, and language-specific template engineering. Metrics focus on F1, exact match, and negative transfer risk. Ablations examine the impact of interpolation weights and typological similarity on performance."
}
